The latest update is out from Zhong An Intelligent Living Service Limited ( (HK:2271) ).

Zhong An Intelligent Living Service Limited has proposed a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.0149 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, equivalent to HKD 0.0169 per share using an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.1355. The dividend remains subject to shareholder approval at a meeting scheduled for 4 June 2026.

If approved, the shares will trade ex-dividend on 11 June 2026, with the book close period running from 15 to 18 June 2026 and a record date of 18 June 2026. Eligible shareholders are expected to receive payment on 9 July 2026 via the Hong Kong share registrar, Tricor Investor Services Limited, underscoring the company’s commitment to returning cash to investors.

More about Zhong An Intelligent Living Service Limited

Zhong An Intelligent Living Service Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company providing intelligent living and related property services. The group focuses on managing residential and possibly mixed-use communities, leveraging technology-driven solutions to enhance daily living and community operations for its customers and property owners.

data.title: Nanshan Aluminium Sets Formal Governance Framework for Audit Committee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited ( (HK:2610) ).

Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited has formally adopted detailed terms of reference for its audit committee, aligning its governance framework with Hong Kong Listing Rules. The committee, established by the board in March 2025, must comprise at least three non-executive directors, with a majority being independent and at least one possessing professional accounting or financial management expertise.

The new terms also restrict former partners of the company’s current auditing firm from serving on the committee for two years after leaving or divesting from the firm, reinforcing auditor independence. Meeting protocols require attendance by the chief financial officer, internal audit head and external auditor representatives, and mandate at least one annual session with internal and external auditors without executive directors present, strengthening oversight and internal control.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2610) stock is a Buy with a HK$71.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2610 Stock Forecast page.

More about Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited

Nanshan Aluminium International Holdings Limited is an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operating in the aluminium industry. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and non-executive directors and adheres to the Listing Rules of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited.

An update from Fujian Holdings Limited ( (HK:0181) ) is now available.

Fujian Holdings Limited reported a decline in revenue to HK$19.1 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, down from HK$24.0 million a year earlier. The loss attributable to shareholders narrowed to HK$15.1 million, compared with a HK$33.1 million loss in 2024, with basic and diluted loss per share improving to 1.32 Hong Kong cents from 2.89 cents.

The results reflect reduced other gains and higher employee expenses, partly offset by lower losses from its associate and reduced operating costs. While the company remains loss-making, the smaller deficit indicates some progress in stabilising operations, which may ease pressure on shareholders compared with the previous year’s significantly larger loss.

data.title: RUSAL Bond Rating Withdrawn After Full Redemption of Exchange-Traded Issue
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has provided an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has announced that the Analytical Credit Rating Agency has withdrawn the credit rating on its series BO-001P-12 exchange-traded bonds, following the full redemption of these uncertificated interest-bearing non-convertible securities. The bonds, previously rated A+(RU) on the Russian national scale, were part of RUSAL’s 001R exchange-traded bond programme, and their redemption closes this specific issue without indicating any downgrade in the issuer’s credit quality.

The technical disclosure, made simultaneously on the Moscow Exchange and the company’s website, underscores RUSAL’s compliance with Russian and Hong Kong listing requirements for timely reporting of rating actions. For bondholders, the withdrawal reflects the completion of obligations on this issue rather than a reassessment of risk, signalling the orderly wind-up of this particular funding instrument in the company’s capital structure.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company and a major player in the global aluminium industry, with its securities listed in Hong Kong, on the Moscow Exchange and SPB Exchange. The company operates under Russian jurisdiction and is a frequent user of the domestic bond market to finance its operations and investments.

The latest update is out from Bilibili, Inc. Class Z ( (HK:9626) ).

Bilibili Inc. has granted 949,277 restricted share units to 102 employees under its Second Amended and Restated 2018 Share Incentive Plan, equal to about 0.23% of its total share capital on a one-share-one-vote basis. The RSUs, granted at no purchase price and based on a market price of HK$182.30 per Class Z share at the grant date, will vest between March 2027 and March 2032 and are subject to clawback provisions for misconduct or termination for cause.

The company says the awards are intended to align employee interests with those of shareholders, strengthen loyalty and retention, and support long-term value creation without imposing additional performance targets. The grants fall within existing scheme limits, do not trigger shareholder approval requirements, and exclude directors, executives, and substantial shareholders, highlighting a focus on incentivizing a broader base of staff while maintaining dilution and governance safeguards.

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0520) ) has shared an announcement.

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management reported a sharp revenue decline of 20.3% in 2025 to RMB3.79 billion, alongside a narrowed but still substantial annual loss attributable to shareholders of RMB300.5 million. The group opened 57 new restaurants but closed 109 underperforming outlets, reflecting a strategic shift toward pruning loss-making sites and realigning locations with its value-for-money positioning amid intense industry competition and more cautious consumer spending.

As of year-end, the company operated 755 Xiabuxiabu and 131 Coucou restaurants in Mainland China, plus a small number of overseas stores, giving it significant scale in a catering market that is recovering only moderately. Management expects that ongoing portfolio adjustments and efficiency measures will help improve restaurant profitability and bolster its position as industry consolidation accelerates, as weaker operators exit and leading brands explore leaner formats and selective overseas expansion.

data.title: Wasion Profit Jumps 50% on Strong Growth in Digital and Smart Grid Businesses
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Wasion Holdings Limited ( (HK:3393) ).

Wasion Holdings Limited reported solid growth for the year ended 31 December 2025, with turnover rising 16% year-on-year to RMB10.07 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders surging 50% to RMB1.06 billion. The improvement was underpinned by higher gross profit, lower impairment losses and reduced finance costs, supporting a proposed final dividend of HK$0.48 per share.

Across its core segments, revenue from Smart Grid Solutions climbed 14% to RMB3.65 billion, AI-integrated energy efficiency solutions grew 9% to RMB2.86 billion and Digital Energy Services jumped 23% to RMB3.56 billion. The performance underscores Wasion’s strengthening position in digital and intelligent energy infrastructure, suggesting improved operating leverage and an expanding role in China’s transition to smarter, more efficient power systems.
